title: Intrinsic Muscles of the Hand
description: OpenStax illustration showcasing the intrinsic muscles of the hand across multiple views (palmar superficial, dorsal deep, and isolated interossei). It details the muscle groups responsible for fine motor control, including the thenar/hypothenar eminences, lumbricals, and interossei.
clinical_notes: Ulnar nerve palsy paralyzes the interossei and medial two lumbricals, leading to a 'claw hand' deformity (hyperextension of MCPs and flexion of IPs). OT intervention includes providing an anti-claw orthosis (lumbrical bar splint) to block MCP hyperextension and restore functional grasp.
